results.rendered_html:
arthya (von artha)
1〉 adj. f. ā  P. 4,4,92 .
a〉 geeignet, angemessen  H. an. 2,344 .  MED. y. 3 . etadanyacca vividhaṃ yuktamarthyamanuṣṭhitam  R. 6,92,77 . vāc  KUMĀRAS. 2,3 .  RAGH. 1,59 . stuti 4,6 . Vgl. anarthya.
— b〉 reich  AK. 3,4,162 . hemārthyaiḥ (yajñaiḥ)  PAÑCAT. I, 347 . tadā hyarthānnṛpe tasminvarṣatyarthye 'nujīviṣu [Page1-0439]  KATHĀS. 23,81 .
— c〉 wonach man verlangt (saṃprārthya)  H. an. 
— d〉 klug, verständig  AK. 3,4,162 .  H. an.  Cit. beim Sch. zu  H. 834 .
— e〉 = dhruva  MED. 
— 2〉 n. Erdharz (śilājatu)  AK. 2,9,104 .  H. 1062 . an.   MED.

results.rendered_html:
arthya a. [arth-karmaṇi ṇyat]
1 Fit to be asked or sought for.
2 [arthādanapetaḥ, artha-yat] Fit, proper, suitable; arthyo virodhaḥ  Mv. 2. 7. 
3 Appropriate, not deviating from the sense, significant; stutyaṃ stutibhirarthyābhirupatasthe sarasvatī  R. 4. 6 , 1. 59 ;  Ku. 2. 3. 
4 Rich, wealthy.
5 Wise, intelligent.
— rthyaṃ Red chalk.
